assignments.title: Introduction to Discrete Mathematics Quiz
assignments.description: The Introduction to Discrete Mathematics Quiz assesses your understanding of the fundamental concepts of discrete mathematics, including sets, relations, and functions. This quiz will cover the basic definitions, properties, and operations of these mathematical structures.
requirements: Read chapters 1-2 of the textbook 'Discrete Mathematics and Its Applications' by Kenneth H. Rosen.
Review the lecture notes and class discussions on the introduction to discrete mathematics.
Complete the practice problems provided in the textbook and class materials.
The quiz will consist of multiple-choice questions, short-answer questions, and problem-solving questions.
You are expected to demonstrate an understanding of the definitions and properties of sets, relations, and functions, as well as basic operations and proofs involving these structures.

assignments.title: Set Theory and Relations Worksheet
assignments.description: Complete the Set Theory and Relations Worksheet by solving problems related to set theory and relations. The worksheet will cover topics such as set operations, relations, and functions. Students are expected to demonstrate their understanding of these concepts by providing step-by-step solutions to the problems.
requirements: Read sections 1.1-1.5 in the textbook 'Discrete Mathematics and Its Applications' by Kenneth H. Rosen to review set theory and relations concepts.
Complete all problems on the Set Theory and Relations Worksheet, showing all work and explanations.
Ensure that all solutions are clearly labeled and organized.
Submit the completed worksheet as a typed document or handwritten with clear, legible writing.

assignments.title: Set Operations and Proofs Checklist
assignments.description: For this assignment, students will complete a checklist to demonstrate their understanding of set operations and proofs. The checklist will cover the following topics: union and intersection of sets, difference and complement of sets, and basic proof techniques. Students should review the relevant sections in the textbook and complete the checklist accurately.
requirements: Define and provide examples of set union, intersection, difference, and complement.
Apply basic proof techniques to verify set equalities.
Complete the checklist accurately and thoroughly.

assignments.title: Set Theory and Proofs Quiz
assignments.description: In this quiz, students will demonstrate their understanding of set theory and proofs by answering a series of questions that test their knowledge of set operations, properties of relations, and basic proof techniques. The quiz will cover topics such as union, intersection, and difference of sets, as well as direct proofs and proof by contrapositive.
requirements: Read sections 2.1-2.3 in the textbook to review set operations and properties of relations.
Complete the provided practice problems on set theory and proofs before taking the quiz.
Answer all questions to the best of your ability, showing work and explanations as needed.
The quiz will consist of multiple-choice, short-answer, and problem-solving questions.

assignments.title: Relations and Functions Worksheet
assignments.description: For this written assignment, students will apply their understanding of relations and functions to solve a series of problems. The worksheet will cover topics such as identifying relations and functions, determining the domain and range of a relation or function, and applying set theory concepts to relations and functions.
requirements: Complete the provided worksheet problems on relations and functions, showing all work and explanations.
Ensure that all answers are clearly labeled and correspond to the correct problem number.
Use proper mathematical notation and terminology throughout the worksheet.
Check all work for accuracy and completeness before submission.

assignments.title: Relations and Functions Quiz
assignments.description: For this quiz, students will demonstrate their understanding of relations and functions in discrete mathematics. The quiz will assess their ability to define and identify relations and functions, understand the properties of relations (reflexive, symmetric, transitive), and apply function concepts (one-to-one, onto, composition).
requirements: Complete a multiple-choice section testing knowledge of relation and function definitions, properties, and types.
Solve short-answer problems that require identifying and explaining properties of given relations and functions.
Work through a few proof-based questions that ask students to verify if a relation is reflexive, symmetric, or transitive, or to determine if a function is one-to-one or onto.

assignments.title: Graph Theory Fundamentals Checklist
assignments.description: For this assignment, students will complete a checklist to demonstrate their understanding of the fundamental concepts in graph theory. The checklist will cover key terms, definitions, and basic properties of graphs, including types of graphs, graph representations, and basic graph algorithms.
requirements: Define and identify basic graph terminology, including vertices, edges, degree, and adjacency.
Recognize and describe different types of graphs, such as simple graphs, weighted graphs, and directed graphs.
Understand and apply basic graph representations, including adjacency matrices and adjacency lists.
Identify and explain basic graph properties, such as connectivity, traversability, and graph isomorphism.
Complete a series of tasks to demonstrate understanding of graph theory fundamentals, including labeling graph components, identifying graph types, and applying basic graph algorithms.

assignments.title: Graph Theory Introduction Quiz
assignments.description: In this quiz, students will demonstrate their understanding of the fundamental concepts of graph theory, including graphs, vertices, edges, degrees, and basic graph terminology. The quiz will assess students' ability to identify and explain key graph theory concepts, as well as their capacity to apply these concepts to simple problems.
requirements: Read chapters 8-10 in the textbook 'Discrete Mathematics and Its Applications' by Kenneth H. Rosen.
Complete the study guide for graph theory, focusing on key terms and concepts.
Take a 30-minute quiz consisting of 10 multiple-choice questions and 2 short-answer questions that cover graph theory basics.
Submit your quiz answers in the designated format.

assignments.title: Graph Traversability and Applications Worksheet
assignments.description: For this worksheet, students will apply graph theory concepts to solve problems related to graph traversability and its applications. Students should read chapters 10 and 11 in the textbook 'Discrete Mathematics and Its Applications' by Kenneth H. Rosen. They will then complete a series of problems that involve determining if a graph is traversable, finding Euler paths and circuits, and applying these concepts to real-world scenarios.
requirements: Read chapters 10 and 11 in the textbook 'Discrete Mathematics and Its Applications' by Kenneth H. Rosen.
Complete the following problems:
Determine if a given graph is traversable.
Find Euler paths and circuits in graphs.
Apply graph traversability concepts to solve real-world problems.
Show all work and explanations for each problem.

assignments.title: Graph Theory Quiz
assignments.description: Complete a quiz that tests your understanding of graph theory concepts covered in week 5, including graph traversability, paths, cycles, and applications. The quiz will consist of multiple-choice questions and short-answer questions that require you to apply graph theory principles to solve problems.
requirements: Review the graph theory concepts covered in week 5, including graph traversability, paths, cycles, and applications.
Complete the quiz within the given time frame, which will be specified by your instructor.
Answer all questions accurately, using the concepts and principles learned in week 5.
For short-answer questions, provide clear and concise solutions, showing all relevant work and explanations.

assignments.title: Combinatorics Fundamentals Checklist
assignments.description: Complete a checklist to demonstrate understanding of the fundamentals of combinatorics. This checklist will cover basic concepts such as permutations, combinations, and basic counting principles. Students should review the relevant sections in the textbook and ensure they can apply these concepts to solve problems.
requirements: Define and explain the concept of permutations and provide examples.
Define and explain the concept of combinations and provide examples.
Apply basic counting principles to solve problems involving permutations and combinations.
Complete all checklist items accurately and thoroughly.

assignments.title: Combinatorics Introduction Quiz
assignments.description: This quiz assesses the student's understanding of combinatorial concepts, including permutations, combinations, and binomial coefficients. Students will be expected to apply these concepts to solve problems, demonstrating their ability to reason and calculate accurately. The quiz will cover the material from Chapter 6 of the textbook, 'Discrete Mathematics and Its Applications' by Kenneth H. Rosen.
requirements: Complete all questions within the allotted time frame
Answer each question to the best of your ability, using the provided space for calculations and explanations as needed
Do not use any external resources or references during the quiz, except for the provided textbook
Ensure all work is shown and explanations are clear for each problem
Submit your quiz as instructed, making sure all pages are included and in the correct order

assignments.title: Advanced Combinatorics Worksheet
assignments.description: For this assignment, students will apply advanced combinatorics concepts to solve complex problems. Using the textbook and class notes, complete the provided worksheet, which includes problems on permutations, combinations, and other advanced combinatorial topics. Show all work and explanations for each problem.
requirements: Complete all problems on the worksheet.
Show detailed work and explanations for each problem.
Use proper mathematical notation and terminology.
Ensure all answers are clearly marked.

assignments.title: Combinatorics Quiz
assignments.description: The student needs to complete a quiz that assesses their understanding of combinatorics concepts covered in week 7, including permutations, combinations, and other counting techniques. The quiz will consist of multiple-choice questions and/or short-answer problems that require the application of combinatorial principles to solve.
requirements: Review the textbook chapters on combinatorics, specifically sections on permutations and combinations.
Complete practice problems from the textbook to reinforce understanding of combinatorial concepts.
Take the quiz within the designated time frame, ensuring to read each question carefully and provide accurate responses.

assignments.title: Final Project
assignments.description: For the final project, you will apply the concepts learned throughout the course to solve a real-world problem or explore a topic of interest in discrete mathematics. Your project should demonstrate a clear understanding of at least three key concepts covered in the course, such as set theory, relations, graph theory, or combinatorics.
requirements: Choose a topic or problem that interests you and is relevant to discrete mathematics.
Provide a clear statement of the problem or topic and explain its significance.
Apply at least three key concepts from the course to analyze or solve the problem.
Include any necessary mathematical derivations, proofs, or algorithms.
Present your findings in a clear and organized manner, using proper mathematical notation and terminology.
The final project should be 5-10 pages in length, not including references or appendices.
Include a bibliography or reference list of sources used in your research.
